code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of university‑related triples into a readable sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language connectors
    verb_map = {
        "city": "is located in",
        "country": "is in",
        "latinName": "has the Latin name",
        "nickname": "is also known as",
        "rector": "has rector",
        "academicStaffSize": "employs",
        "established": "was established in",
        "state": "lies in the state of",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "hasToItsNortheast": "has to its northeast",
        "dean": "has dean",
        "location": "is located at",
        "numberOfStudents": "has",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "director": "has director",
        "motto": "has the motto",
        "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": "has",
        "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": "has",
        "officialSchoolColour": "has official colours",
        "outlookRanking": "holds an Outlook ranking of",
        "sportsOffered": "offers sport",
        "wasGivenTheTechnicalCampusStatusBy": "was given the technical campus status by",
        "founder": "was founded by",
        "leader": "is led by",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "religion": "practices",
        "headquarter": "has headquarters in",
        "largestCity": "has largest city",
        "river": "has river",
        "hasToItsWest": "has to its west",
        "anthem": "has anthem",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "legislature": "has legislature",
        "sportGoverningBody": "has sport governing body",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "neighboringMunicipality": "neighbors",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "patronSaint": "has patron saint",
        "hasToItsNorthwest": "has to its northwest",
        "campus": "has campus",
        "president": "has president",
        "staff": "has staff",
        "numberOfDoctoralStudents": "has",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is at elevation",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
        "longName": "has long name",
    }

    phrases = []
    for t in triples:
        verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, "has")
        phrase = f"{t.subject} {verb} {t.object}"
        phrases.append(phrase)

    sentence = ", ".join(phrases) + "."
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
